words.definition:
Relating to or deriving from Asia.
There are differences between African and Asiatic lions.

The capital city of Nunavut.
The city of Iqaluit was formerly known as Frobisher Bay.
A famous Hungarian composer. Franz Liszt was known for his piano compositions.

A person of mixed indigenous and Euro-Canadian ancestry.
Louis Riel was a political leader of the
Métis people of the Canadian Prairies.

The capital city of Manitoba.
The city of Winnipeg started as a trading post for the Hudson’s Bay Company.

A vehicle that floods the surface to clean the ice in an arena.
I have always wanted to drive a
Zamboni at a hockey game.

To solemnly renounce a belief or cause. Edward VIII decided to abjure the throne in order to marry the woman of
his choice.

An area damaged by scraping or wearing away.
He got an abrasion on his knee when he
fell off the slide.

To declare a person free from guilt or wrongdoing.
The lawyer tried to absolve his client of
any blame.

Difficult to understand.
The books in the antique bookshop were abstruse.

Enthusiastic and public praise.
She won acclaim for her commitment to the environment.

The act of getting the position without an election due to being the only candidate.
Three of the positions on the student council were appointed by acclamation.

To make someone aware of or familiar with.
The dog sitter had to acquaint herself
with the dog’s habits.

The ability to make good judgements.
The store owner’s business acumen
helped make him successful.

Lying near or close by; adjoining.
His best friend lived in the house
adjacent to his.

A natural attraction to a person or liking for a thing.
He was fun to watch in the rodeo because of his strong affinity with horses.

A plant widely cultivated as a forage crop.
Many farmers grow alfalfa for animal
feed.

A union formed by agreement; a joining of interests.
The European Union is a trade alliance.

An annual calendar containing important dates and statistical information.
The fisherman might consult the
almanac for tide tables.

Available as another possibility or choice.
Épelle-Moi is a French alternative to
the English spelling bee.

To change the form of something, such as a law, by addition or deletion.
The committee will amend the proposal
before we vote on it.

A cold-blooded vertebrate adapted to life in the water or on land.
The bull frog is my favourite
amphibian.

An X-ray picture of blood vessels, used to show any abnormality in the flow of blood.
An angiogram is usually performed in a
hospital.

Very great pain or grief.
The girl felt great anguish over losing her dog.

A product that destroys or weakens harmful micro-organisms.
Penicillin is an antibiotic commonly
used to fight infections.

Too old for work, service, or use.
Their old computer was antiquated and no longer ran programmes well.

A punctuation mark used to show the omission of letters or to indicate possession.
In writing, we use an apostrophe “s” to show possession.

An array of resources.
We have an arsenal of computers at our disposal.

A chronic breathing disorder.
A person with asthma has trouble breathing, especially on humid days.

To gradually decline in ability due to underuse or neglect.
The imagination can atrophy from lack
of use.

An accomplishment; something that is achieved.
Everyone commended him for his impressive attainment in winning the
spelling bee.

Hair that is reddish brown or golden-brown in colour.
She donated her long auburn hair for a
wig for cancer patients.

A distinctive atmosphere or pervasive quality that seems to come from a person.
The person who got the job exuded an aura of confidence throughout the interview.

Filled with wonder and admiration.
I was awestruck by the beauty and grandeur of the Grand Canyon.

A pointed tool used for making small holes in leather or wood.
Use an awl to make another hole in your belt.

Rawhide thongs or lacings.
Canadians often use babiche in making snowshoes.

A marine crustacean that spends its entire adult life attached to some underwater object.
We saw a huge barnacle stuck to the rock during low tide.

A clay-like mineral or rock.
The chief use of bauxite is in the manufacture of aluminum.

To resent or be dissatisfied with; to envy someone’s possessions.
I couldn’t begrudge her the new cell
phone as she worked so hard for it.

A structure designed to be opened on several sides to afford a wide view.
We overlooked the city from a
belvedere.

Deprived or lacking something.
Because the room was bereft of colour it appeared cold and sterile.

Cruel or savage; of or like a beast.
The bestial zodiac signs include the bull and the ram.

A group of birds or animals.
We saw a bevy of quail on our walk.

Occurring twice a year.
The family liked to take a biannual
vacation.

Having two axes;
A biaxial graph has both a vertical and horizontal axis.

Having to do with two languages; able to speak two languages fluently.
Canada is a bilingual country.

An area or region that constitutes a natural ecological community.
Each bioregion has a distinct flora, fauna and ecosystem from those of other areas.

A small, soft round cake like a scone.
For breakfast I like sausage and egg on a biscuit.

Brazenly obvious; forcing one’s attention.
His loud behaviour was a blatant
attempt to disturb us.

Carried or transported.
In pollination, some seeds are borne by the wind.

A short, stiff hair especially on an animal’s back, often used for making brushes.
A bristle came out of the paint brush
and stuck to the wall.

A vegetable that resembles a tree in shape; a variety of cabbage.
It is probably safe to say that broccoli is
not the favourite vegetable of most children.

A pamphlet or leaflet, especially one giving descriptive information.
The travel agent gave us a brochure
about Florida.

A company providing the services of an intermediary, acting between a buyer and seller.
I have a meeting at the insurance
brokerage to review my policy.

Toasted bread drizzled with olive oil and usually topped with diced tomatoes.
He prefers garlic bread to bruschetta
because he is not fond of tomatoes.

A heavily-built wild ox, with backswept horns.
A buffalo is found mostly in Old World tropics.

Shaped like a bulb; rounded and/or swollen.
Garlic is a bulbous herb whose cloves
are used to flavour food.

A grant of money to a student at a college or university.
The bursary will help pay my tuition
expenses.

A unit of volume, equal to just over 35 litres, or a container of such capacity.
I would like to buy a bushel of apples.

Excited activity and movement.
She loved the hustle and bustle of the city streets.

A secret political clique or faction.
The cabal of dissidents planed the coup to overthrow the government.

To persuade using flattery or promises. My mother tried to cajole my brother into helping by telling him she needed
his muscles.

A soft, silver-white element which naturally occurs in bones.
Milk is an excellent source of calcium.

Slander; a false statement designed to injure the reputation of someone or something.
Although the scientist received a lot of calumny in the newspaper, he pressed on with his research.

A woman’s waist-length, sleeveless under-garment with shoulder straps. My blouse was sheer so I wore a
camisole underneath it.

Concealment that alters or obscures the appearance.
Drab plumage provides female birds with camouflage against predators.

A series of connected activities planned to achieve some goal.
We planned a campaign to raise funds
for new computers.
The power or ability to do something. Watching the runner’s practice really showed his capability to win a medal.

A small container, usually round or cylindrical.
The students buried a time capsule on the school grounds to be opened years
from now.

A wide-mouthed glass or metal bottle for holding serving beverages.
I broke the carafe for my coffee maker.

A knitted sweater opening down the front.
She selected a long cardigan to go with
her dress.

A usually comic representation of a person by exaggeration of characteristics in a picture, writing or mime.
At our school fair we had an artist that
could quickly draw a caricature of a subject but still capture a good likeness.

Long-stalked, fragrant flowers in a variety of colours.
A carnation is often worn by both men
and women in a bridal party.

A merry-go-round; or a rotating conveyer system, especially for passenger luggage at an airport.
Although I checked the carousel, my suitcase was definitely not on it.

The trade or art of building wooden objects or structures.
Because I like to do woodwork, I’m
planning to study carpentry in college.

A sudden, widespread or extraordinary disaster.
The flood was a catastrophe, leaving
thousands of people homeless.

A type of basin or reservoir for catching drainage.
The rainwater collected in the
catchment basin.

The wormlike larva of a certain butterfly or a moth.
The caterpillar turned into a beautiful
butterfly.

A person who plays the cello which is the second largest member of the violin family.
Yo Yo Ma is a famous cellist.

A shiny, transparent substance made from cellulose, used to package things.
Wrap the sandwiches in cellophane.

A place for burying the dead; a graveyard.
A superstition says to whistle while you
walk past the cemetery.

Periods of one hundred years.
The medieval castle had stood for centuries until it was levelled by an earthquake.

An occasion celebrating a particular event or achievement.
The whole family came to the wedding
ceremony.
A fixture with branches for lights, usually hanging from the ceiling. Because of the hanging crystals, it is always a chore to clean the chandelier.

A pretense intended to create a respectable appearance.
His pretending to study was just a
charade.

A person whose work is driving an automobile for others.
The chauffeur will drive you to school.

Stylish and elegant in dress or appearance.
She wore a chic dress for the wedding.

A light, transparent fabric.
Her chiffon dress floated and swirled as she danced.

A cutting tool with a sharp edge at the end of a strong blade.
The carpenter used the chisel to shape
the sculpture.

An electronically generated caption superimposed on a television screen. The chyron on the newscast identified
the person being interviewed.

A small, exclusive group of people. The mother tried to convince her daughter to avoid becoming part of a
clique when she started high school.

An addition that explains or modifies a will or part of one.
The lawyer wrote a codicil to his client’s will.

Extremely large or great.
People often refer to an elephant as being colossal.

An expression of opinion.
The journalist wrote a commentary
about the current state of politics.

The natural texture, colour and appearance of the skin, especially the face.
She has a beautiful peaches and cream
complexion.
Intended or likely to be calming. The teacher used a conciliatory approach to settle the playground dispute.

A number of things or parts forming a group or mass.
Rogers Communications is perhaps the
largest conglomerate in Canada.

The compulsory enlistment of people in the armed forces; draft.
Canada used a form of conscription to
enlist people during World War II.

A system of parallel mountain ranges.
A good example of cordillera is in the Rockies.
Too ready to believe something.  Never be so credulous that you believe everything you are told.

The scientific study of crime.
In criminology, we studied illegal behaviour and the penal system.

Dark, purplish-red.
The actress wore a crimson dress to the gala.

Discouraging; intimidating.
Each labour of Hercules was a daunting task.

To walk slowly or idly; to delay; to waste time.
The boy was dawdling on his way to school.

A first appearance.
The film marked his debut as a director.

A trick meant to deceive; a fraud; a sham.
The story he told his mother in order to go to the movies was clearly a
deception.
Something delightful or pleasing, especially rare or expensive food. Escargot is a French delicacy.

Temporarily out of one’s senses; raving.
The high fever made him delirious.

A mark against a person’s record given for unsatisfactory performance or behaviour.
The teacher gave him another demerit
for his bad behaviour.

A noun used to denote the inhabitants of a particular country, state, etc.
The demonym for people who live in
Canada is Canadian.

A person or animal that is later in the same family tree of another.
Queen Elizabeth I was a descendant of
Henry VIII.

A determination of the cause or nature of an illness.
We hope to hear the doctor’s diagnosis
today.

Training, especially of the mind or character.
The new recruits were subjected to
regular discipline.

Essentially different in kind; without comparison or relation.
My grandmother and grandfather had very disparate interests, yet they were
very happily married.

To cut into pieces; to cut apart.
I hope we do not dissect anything in
biology class today.

To disclose; to reveal a secret.
Her friend promised not to divulge the contents of our conversation.

An area ruled by one person.
The king’s domain stretched from sea to sea.

That which can be doubted; uncertain.
He was very dubious about his chances of winning the spelling bee.

Pliant and flexible.
Because silver is ductile, a jeweller is able to draw it out into a thin wire.

A dark underground room in which prisoners are kept.
We expected the ancient castle to have
a dungeon.

To rub out; to destroy or wipe out.
Over time, exposure to the elements may efface inscriptions on monuments.
A sculpture or model of a person. Canadian coins bear an effigy of the monarch on the face.

A select group that is superior in terms of ability.
After the tryouts the best players were selected to make up the elite hockey
team.

To give off; to emit.
A scent will often emanate from a flower.

A person sent on a special mission, usually diplomatic.
Canada sent an emissary to Greenland.
The production and discharge of something, especially gas or radiation. The Paris Accord was designed to reduce the emission of greenhouse gases, among other things.

A male who is the sovereign ruler of an empire.
There is a classic story of an emperor who was deceived into believing he had wonderful new clothes.

To try to equal or excel, usually with considerable effort.
A parable tells us to emulate the
industry of the ant.

A plant or animal native to a certain place.
A kangaroo is endemic to Australia.

To occupy wholly; to take up all the attention of.
This is a story that will completely
engross you.

An action or undertaking, especially a bold or complex one.
The business was a joint enterprise with
his partner.

To form a mental picture of.
The architect was able to envisage the completed building.

A short quotation at the beginning of a book or chapter intended to suggest its theme.
The epigraph was chosen to entice the
reader.

A short statement in memory of a dead person.
We found the epitaph carved on the
tombstone.

At equal distances.
The two cities are equidistant from here.

Relating to or designed for efficiency and comfort in the work place.
The snow shovel’s new ergonomic
design puts less strain on the back during snow removal.

Edible snails that are considered a delicacy.
He ordered escargots as an appetizer to
impress his date.

To deliberately avoid using.
He appealed to the protestors to
eschew the use of violence.

Particularly; chiefly.
Her new book is designed especially for young readers.

That which makes a thing what it is; the necessary part or parts.
Kindness is the essence of politeness.

An informed guess.
He made an estimation of how much he needed to save to buy the new video game.

A speech or writing in honour of a person who has died.
My uncle gave a fine eulogy at his
friend’s funeral.

The removal of people from a place of danger.
The evacuation of the people in the burning apartment was carried out swiftly.

A large water jug with a wide mouth and spout.
The archaeologist found pieces of an
ancient ewer at the excavation site.

Unusually good in quality.
The inn was famous for the excellence
of its food.

An expulsion of breath.
It was so cold that my exhalation
created a little cloud in the air.

To free someone or something from a difficulty.
They had to extricate the ship from the
sandbar.

Uncultivated; ploughed and harrowed but left unsown.
The farmer decided to leave that field
fallow this year and plant it next year.

Belief that everything that happens is inevitable.
A believer in fatalism does not think
that his actions can change things.

A tap.
The sink overflowed when she left the
faucet running.

Pertaining to a woman or a girl.
The store was filled with wonderfully
feminine fashions for spring.

Fierce, savage, widely cruel or destructive.
The ferocious snowstorm grounded all
the planes.

Imaginary, unreal; not genuine.
He gave a fictitious address, which was actually an empty lot.

The fact of being final finished or settled.
He spoke with such finality that there
was no point in debating the issue.

Having to do with money matters. We went to see a financial planner
after winning the lottery.

Highly noticeable, glaring, notorious. The disruptive teenagers were reprimanded for their flagrant
disregard of the rules.

Minor weakness or failings of character.
It is the many human foibles that
provide ideas for most writers.

Irritable and quarrelsome.
They fight and squabble like fractious
children who are over-tired.

The privilege of providing a company’s service in a given area by individual owners.
The businessmen decided to open a Tim
Horton’s franchise.

Deceitful; dishonest.
The man obtained the painting by
fraudulent means.

Affected by great anxiety or stress, upset.
The girls were fraught with worry as they sat on the bench outside of the
principal’s office.

The art of painting with water colours on damp, fresh plaster.
The ceiling of the Sistine Chapel is an example of a famous fresco.

Easily crumbled.
Feta cheese is friable.

The action of one surface or object rubbing against another.
A rash can occur where there is friction
of clothes rubbing against the skin.

Extremely cold.
In spite of the bright sun, there was still a frigid wind blowing from the north.

In working order.
Although antiquated, my old VCR is still
functional.

A small mechanical or electronic device or tool.
The technophile always wanted to have
the latest gadget.

Noble, brave or daring.
The firemen made a gallant effort to save all the residents and pets from the burning building.

A large, heavy sailing ship, usually having three or four decks.
In the 16th century, the Spaniards used a galleon to bring treasure back from
the New World.

The entire range of anything.
In one minute, I ran the gamut of emotions from hope to despair.

A very thin, light cloth often used to dress wounds.
Cotton gauze is often used for
bandages.

A willingness to share with others; unselfishness.
The lawyer’s donation to the museum
was an act of generosity.

Pleasant, cheerful and friendly.
He was promoted to customer service manager in part because of his genial manner.

People of good family and social position.
In historical fiction, the gentry would be seen to live in large manor houses with a lot of staff.
The branch of mathematics that deals with lines, angles, surfaces and solids. The area of mathematics that I find most difficult is geometry.
A plant with large, bright flowers. The geranium was planted in a large earthenware pot.

To begin to grow or develop.
We will germinate lima beans as part of the life sciences unit in science class.

A device, scheme or strategy used to attract attention; a stunt.
The store owner hired clowns as a
gimmick to attract customers.

A short, quick view.
We had a glimpse of the falls as we passed by them.

A small, pointed beard on a man’s chin. Colonel Sanders had a goatee in all his
pictures.

A place or building where grain is stored.
Kernels of wheat were evidence that
the old building was used as a granary.

A soft form of carbon with a metallic lustre and a greasy feel.
What we call the lead in pencils is
actually graphite.

Tough tissue full of cartilage.
The meat was tough because it was mostly gristle.

A metal eyelet.
There was a grommet at the corner of the tarp to prevent it from ripping.

A person who maintains a sports ground or park.
The groundskeeper raked the baseball
diamond before each game.

To behave in a servile or obedient manner.
Do you expect me to grovel to him?

Easily deceived or cheated.
My brother is so gullible that he believes everything he sees on television.

Exercises and movements that demonstrate strength, balance and agility.
The school team gave a display of
gymnastics.

Regarded as holy or sacred.
They were quiet in the hallowed halls of the cathedral.

A mythical monster.
In old Greek stories, a harpy punished those who were violent to others.

A young cow that has not yet had a calf.
The heifer stood in the pasture with her tail to the wind and rain.

Half of a sphere or globe.
Canada is a country in the northern
hemisphere.

Able to be passed down from generation to generation.
Her green eyes were hereditary, as they were just like her mother’s and her
grandmother’s.

To cut with an axe or sword.
The pioneers had to hew logs into beams to build their houses.

To save and store away.
A squirrel will hoard nuts throughout the fall to eat all winter.
Respect, reverence and honour. Everyone paid homage to the great leader.

A word that is pronounced the same as another but that differs in meaning.
Hoard is a homophone of horde.

Benevolent, compassionate, kind and caring.
There are laws promoting the humane
treatment of animals.

In a quick or hasty manner.
She did her homework hurriedly because she wanted to go out with her friends.

Wild goats with semicircular horns. The herd of ibex grazed in the cool of
the early morning.

To inspire.
He hopes to imbue his son with the ambition to succeed.

Living forever; never dying.
Many superheroes and cartoon characters are immortal.

Flawless or faultless.
My father’s grooming is impeccable; he
never seems to have a hair out of place.

Suggested though not directly expressed.
It is implicit, though not actually said, that we should be quiet in a place of
worship.

A person who assumes a false name or character.
The guard’s place was taken by an
imposter.

To imprison or confine.
Until recent times, a court could
incarcerate a debtor.

Never stopping; continued or repeated without interruption.
The roar of Niagara Falls is incessant.

To encourage or urge on.
If the union’s position is not acknowledged by management, it could incite a strike.

To compensate or reimburse for harm or loss.
The court ordered the insurance company to indemnify them for losses
caused by the accident.

Childish behaviour.
Fighting at major league games is an
example of infantilism.

Easily transmitted from one person to another; communicable.
A cold is a very infectious ailment.
To enter or gain access surreptitiously. Undercover policemen will try to infiltrate gangs to prove criminal behaviour.

Not easily bent; stiff; rigid.
We used a steel rod that was inflexible.

The quality of being clever, original and inventive.
Software designers display great
ingenuity.

To get on the good side of someone by flattering or trying to pleasing them.
The new girl tried to ingratiate herself
into the clique.

The internal workings of the body or any complex mechanism.
We saw the innards of the machine
when the technician opened it.

To vaccinate against a disease.
The doctor’s office made sure to have enough vaccine to inoculate everyone during flu season.

A badge or distinguishing mark of membership of an organization; symbols of rank or authority.
The stripes on an officer’s uniform are
an insignia of rank.

Without much taste.
A mixture of milk and water is an
insipid drink.

The inability to sleep, especially when ongoing.
She is often tired at work because she
suffers from insomnia.

Of or having to do with islands or islanders.
Vancouver Island enjoys a moderate,
insular climate.

A hormone the body needs to use sugar and carbohydrates.
Dr. Banting and Charles Best discovered
insulin, which is used to treat diabetes.

Necessary to complete the whole thing; belonging as part of the whole.
The enthusiastic new employee quickly became an integral part of the
company.

To intervene in order to bring about an agreement; to mediate.
I will try to intercede in their argument.

To arouse interest or curiosity.
She presented her proposal in order to intrigue the other members of the group.

Belonging to something by its very nature; essential.
The ultimate goal of character education is to develop people’s intrinsic motivation to do the right
thing.

A very small quantity.
There is not an iota of truth to your story.

The repetition of a process or utterance.
In the afternoon announcements, the principal repeated his iteration about
staying out of the mud puddles.

A large spotted or black predatory cat found in the rainforest.
The jaguar is a beautiful, graceful
animal.

Each of a series of parallel supporting beams.
One more joist and the roof will be
ready.
A thick, tangled forest in a hot country. Tarzan lived in the jungle.

The good or bad emanations felt to be generated by someone or something. Many of my friends sent good karma my way when my mother was in the
hospital.
A type of terrain with sinkholes, caverns and underground streams. We visited the karst topography north of Kingston.
Recognized; familiar; famous. It is a known fact that he is a millionaire.

The bone at a finger joint.
When I banged my hand on the doorjamb, I bruised my knuckle.

A game played between two ten-player teams using a netted stick and a rubber ball.
Canada’s national game, lacrosse
requires speed and agility.

A patterned drapery or upholstery fabric similar to brocade.
She chose a lovely lampas to re-cover
the chair.

Present or existing and capable of development.
She has a latent talent for music.

Heavy; hard to lift or move.
At the end of the race, the tired runner’s legs felt leaden.

A tall stand with a sloping top.
The principal stood at the lectern to call the names at graduation.

A group of persons having the duty and power to make laws.
Each Canadian province and territory
has its own legislature.

A very large aquatic creature.
A whale is sometimes called a leviathan
of the deep.

The text of an opera or other long musical work.
Was it Gilbert or Sullivan who wrote the
libretto for “The Pirates of Penzance”?

Happily, cheerfully, gladly or with pleasure.
The businessperson was as lief to join one certain service club or another.

In place of; instead.
He had to write an essay in lieu of doing the exam.

A band of strong tissue that connects bones.
The football player damaged a
ligament in his knee when he was tackled.

Probability.
The more you study, the greater the
likelihood that you’ll get good grades.

A large, luxurious automobile often with a partition behind the driver.
He took his girlfriend to the prom in a
limousine.

A locking pin inserted through a hold in the end of an axle to keep the wheel on.
The wheel came off when the linchpin
broke.

Pertaining to literature, especially good writing.
The teacher suggested that they could
enjoy graphic novels, but that they should also read some literary works.

Topsoil.
In the spring, he will replant the flowers in the garden using fresh loam.

A railway engine.
In the stories of “Thomas the Tank Engine”, Thomas is a steam
locomotive.

A type of candy on the end of a stick.
Mother always told me not to run with a lollipop in my mouth.

Easy to understand.
She provided a lucid explanation of a
difficult topic.

Pain in the muscles and joints of the lower back.
The doctor diagnosed her pain as being
lumbago.

A formal lunch, usually in connection with a meeting.
The awards were presented at a
luncheon.
A grey black magnetic mineral. An important form of iron ore is magnetite.

To cause permanent damage to, or loss of, a part of the body.
He was permanently maimed by the
accident.

A compound of oils used as a butter substitute.
I prefer margarine to butter on my
toast.

To soak food, especially meat or fish, in a seasoned liquid, usually containing oil and/or vinegar.
If you marinate the steak, it will be
tender.

Legally or formally recognized union of two people as partners in a personal relationship.
We wished the bride and groom a long and happy marriage.

The science of diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of disease.
Her goal was to study medicine at university to become a children’s
doctor.

A form of drama with exaggerated emotion and limited characterization. We watched the melodrama with
Kleenex boxes on our laps.

A historical account of facts or events written from personal knowledge.
Someday I would like to write my
memoirs.

An unhealthy or unpleasant smell.
Many years ago, it was believed that a
miasma caused disease.

A solid, naturally occurring inorganic substance.
Many mineral products, including iron and nickel, have been important to the
Canadian economy.

A small whale.
She recognized it as a minke whale because of its dark gray back and markings on its fins.

An optical illusion, usually in the desert or at sea.
We often hear stories of people lost in
the desert seeing a mirage of water.

Action or conduct that causes trouble or harm, often not intentionally.
A child’s mischief with matches may
cause a serious fire.

An independent unit that forms part of a larger structure or programme.
When Neil Armstrong went to the
moon, they used the lunar module.

The cultivation of a single crop in a given area.
You can ruin the soil with continuous
monoculture.

An instance of change or alteration.
The plant in the lab underwent a
strange mutation.

A particle between one and one hundred nanometres in size.
A nanoparticle is a microscopic speck.

Natural and unaffected; innocent.
She was naïve to believe their promises.

Having to do with the lowest level of a high tide in a monthly period.
The neap tides occur twice a month at the first and last quarters of the moon.

Hazy; indistinct; vague.
It is often said that there is a nebulous distinction between bravery and foolishness.

A note or group of notes to be sung to a single syllable.
Gregorian chants were the originator of
the neume.

A short novel or narrative story.
People who are pressed for time like to read a novella.

To wipe out.
The solar eclipse will temporarily
obliterate our view of the sun.

The state of being completely forgotten.
After she stopped making movies, the actress’ name and reputation fell into
oblivion.

Noticeable or prominent in an unwelcome way.
The obtrusive cough interrupted our
enjoyment of the movie.

A semi-precious type of agate with layers of different colours.
I had a new cameo made from black
onyx.

A person who chooses to take the favourable view of things.
An optimist sees a glass of water as
half full rather than half empty.

A person who specializes in the examination of the eyes for defects in visions and prescribes corrective measures.
The optometrist prescribed new glasses for the student.

A dentist who specializes in irregularities in the teeth and jaws. The orthodontist put braces on the
girl’s teeth.

An elaborate spectacle or a procession in costume.
The coronation of the new king was a
splendid pageant.

Pertaining to a disease that is prevalent over a whole country or the world.
In 1918, Spanish flu was pandemic.

A wide, unbroken view of a surrounding region.
We enjoyed the panorama from the top
of the hill.
A large, yellow-skinned tropical fruit. We like to eat papaya as a special treat.

A short, simple story used to teach some truth or moral lesson.
He told the children the parable of the
boy who cried wolf.

The father, or male head, of a family or tribe.
In some cultures, the patriarch is the
final authority.

An act of presenting a play, concert or other production.
The class practised and practised for their performance in the school concert.

To bleach hair.
She planned to peroxide her hair to lighten it.

Childishly sulky or bad tempered.
The petulant athlete mouthed off at the referee.

A young pike; a type of fish.
The young boy was proud of himself when he caught a pickerel.

One of a family of birds with a large body and small head.
The pigeon pecked at the crumbs on
the ground.

Having leaflets arranged in pairs on opposite sides of the stem.
Some ferns have pinnate fronds.

A slender tube attached to a bulb for measuring out small quantities of liquid.
They used a pipette to transfer liquids
in the laboratory experiment.

The illegal use or reproduction of another person’s work.
Downloading music without paying for
it is a form of piracy.

A person who brings a case against another into court.
The plaintiff sued his neighbour when
his child maliciously broke the window.

Microscopic organisms in water.
Shellfish feed on plankton.

To plunge or drop quickly and suddenly.
As the roller coaster began to plummet
downwards, I raised my hands in the air.
The art and science of government. Being interested in how the government works, he chose a career in politics.

Relating to a figure with five or more sides.
In geometry class, they had to draw a
polygonal shape.

Domesticated birds such as chickens and turkeys.
While he does not eat red meat, he will
eat poultry.

To flow rapidly in a steady stream.
Come and watch the rain pouring off the glass roof.

A large open area of grassland.
The Canadian prairie is one of the most productive wheat fields in the world.

Introductory; preparatory.
The inexperienced boxer fought in a
preliminary bout before challenging the champion.

Highly esteemed; well respected for one’s achievements.
We were lucky to get such a prestigious
speaker at our conference.

The head of a school or other educational institution.
Our school got a new principal this
year.

Competent or skilled in doing or using something.
By taking classes, he became proficient
in the use of computers.

Producing many works.
The prolific author wrote at least two books every year.

A feather-footed bird, native to northern regions.
The ptarmigan is the official bird of
Nunavut.

A star-like object thought to be most distant and most luminous in the universe.
The astronomer studied the quasar
though his telescope.

To extinguish; to quiet or allay.
The mother quelled the baby’s cries by rocking him and singing a lullaby.

A tortilla filled with cheese, and heated.
A quesadilla is basically a grilled cheese sandwich on a tortilla instead of bread.

Swiftness or speed.
The goalie moved with great rapidity to block the shot.

A connection or relationship, especially agreeable.
The teacher has an excellent rapport
with her students.

Very hungry, famished.
The hikers were all ravenous by the time they stopped to eat.

A person who receives or accepts something.
She was very proud to be the recipient
of the Principal’s Award.

To identify a person or thing as previously seen or known.
I could scarcely recognize my old friend because I hadn’t seen her in such a long
time.
To correct or make right; to amend. He tried to rectify his error in filling the order.

To strengthen or support.
They had to reinforce the basement after the flood weakened it.

Surrender or resign a right or possession.
The prince decided to relinquish his
claim to the throne.

Slow to act due to unwillingness.
He was very reluctant to enter the cold water.

Intended as a remedy or improvement.
Students can go to the math clinic after school for remedial help.

To give or to provide.
The teacher was always ready to render
assistance when it was needed.

To give up formally; to renounce; to disclaim.
In order to keep peace in the family, she
decided to renounce her claims to the estate.

The act of sending back or restoring to one’s own country.
Many people stood on the bridge over the Highway of Heroes to watch the repatriation of the fallen Canadian
soldiers.

An indirect influence or reaction from an event.
Having to replace all her identification was just one repercussion of having her
purse stolen.

To repeat; to make a copy of.
No one was able to replicate his
experiment.

Relief or/from impending punishment or hardship, often temporary.
The lull in the storm offered the
residents only a temporary reprieve.

A rebuke or censure.
He received a reproach from his teacher for his lack of effort.

To revoke or cancel.
The teacher threatened to rescind our field trip if we didn’t behave.

A small amount of something that remains after the main part is gone. You should always wash fruit to get rid of the residue of any pesticides that
were used.

To bring back from obscurity or disrepair.
The Historical Society wants to
resurrect the historic building to its former glory.

A survey of past time or events.
In retrospect, the first procedure seems better.

Income, especially of a large amount from any source.
The revenue the government gets from taxes is used for things like health care.

A vitamin of the B complex.
Liver, milk and eggs are especially good sources of riboflavin.

Absurd, laughable; not reasonable.
It is ridiculous to think that one person can do the work of seven.

A breeding colony of seabirds, wading birds, seals or turtles.
The seals were gathered in the rookery
at the water’s edge.

Noisy and disorderly.
The supply teacher complained that the class was rowdy.

To give up one thing in favour of another.
Sometimes we have to sacrifice convenience for lowering our carbon footprint.

A giant cactus native to Mexico and the south western United States.
It takes a saguaro ten years to grow its
first one and a half inches.

A learned person, having much knowledge; a student.
He is a scholar of fine art.

An official of an organization who conducts its correspondence and keeps records.
The student council has a secretary
who records the minutes of the meetings.

A device which detects a physical property.
A motion sensor turns the lights on
when we get home.

Brownish in colour.
She printed the picture of her son and his grandfather in sepia tone.

A succession of things or events; the order in which events occur.
She read the Harry Potter series in the
sequence of their publication.

Lac resin melted into flakes and used for making varnish.
I gave the old table a thick coat of
shellac.

High-spirited behaviours; mischief.
The children were up to all kinds of
shenanigans over the weekend.

In a manner which is genuine in feeling; honest straightforward.
He sincerely regretted not being able to attend his best friend’s wedding.

Nervous and shy; easily startled.
Our young horse was skittish in its new surroundings.

Used to express good health to a friend before a drink.
The custom of clinking glasses and saying “skoal” may come from a Viking tradition to ensure to their drink hadn’t been poisoned.

Any of various card games played by one person.
He often played solitaire to pass the
time.

To sprinkle or deposit seeds into the soil.
The farmer was sowing seeds into the ground.

A kind of grass.
You can find spartina growing in salt marshes.

To produce or generate, especially in large amounts.
Frogs spawn many tadpoles.

Dramatically beautiful or strikingly large.
We were treated to spectacular
fireworks on Canada Day.

Not planned.
While on vacation, the family made a spontaneous decision to visit Disney World.

A period of inactivity.
His writer’s block led to a temporary
stasis.

The job of taking care of something.
The caretaker has the stewardship of the school.

An ending added to a word to give another meaning.
The suffix “ly” often indicates an
adverb.

Of or having to do with a person or thing taking the place of another.
The common cowbird lays her eggs in the nest of another bird, which will act
as the surrogate mother.

A word that means the same as another word.
Shut is a synonym of close.

A mineral with a soapy feel, often used for powder or lubricants.
I like to use talcum powder after I’ve
had a shower.

A starchy substance in hard, white grains.
She prefers tapioca pudding to rice
pudding.

A large hairy spider.
The bite of a tarantula is painful but not that serious.

Of rope or muscles that are tight, not slack.
He made sure that the ropes holding the mattress to the roof of his car were
taut.
The state of being tedious or tiresome. The tedium of the reading material was putting her to sleep.

The quality of being very determined.
Her tenacity helped her to become an elite athlete.

In Canada, a region that has not been admitted as a province but has its own legislature.
Nunavut is the newest Canadian
territory.

Completely; doing all that should be done.
Please wash your hands thoroughly
before supper.

A written or recorded copy.
The school sent me a transcript of my marks.

The act of betraying one’s country or ruler.
The man’s disloyal actions against the government resulted in his arrest for
treason.

A marine sporting fish of the indo-pacific.
They use trevally for making sashimi.
Of little value or importance. Sometimes the media plays up trivial events and ignores the bigger issues.

A large brass wind instrument with a sliding tube.
He plays the trombone in the orchestra.

A soft porous rock from a volcanic eruption.
I have several samples of volcanic tuff
in my rock collection.

Disorderly, characterized by commotion or disturbance.
The seagull rookery was a tumultuous
scene with thousands of birds all squawking at once.

The fully shaded inner region of a shadow that is cast by an opaque object.
The artist tried to capture the umbra of the shadow cast by the vase.

Deprived; not enjoying the normal standard of living in a society.
I did not feel underprivileged using a
bike instead of a car.

Not missing the intended target.
The hawk was unerring as it swooped down on the mouse.

A violent or sudden change or disruption.
The earthquake caused an upheaval in
people’s lives.

Someone who shows people to their seats in a theatre, church, etc.
The usher led me to an aisle seat.

Fur obtained from red squirrels.
In the 13th and 14th century vair was used as trimming for clothing.

A means of suppling fresh air.
The kitchen needs more adequate
ventilation to remove cooking odours.

The process of establishing the accuracy of something.
Newspapers go through a verification process before a story is printed.

Keeping careful watch for possible danger.
The burglar was spotted by vigilant
neighbours.

To speak ill of; to slander.
Even if you disagree with him, you shouldn’t vilify him.

A plantation of grapevines.
That vineyard produces many grapes.

A type of plastic.
The siding on our house is made of
vinyl.

Determination.
She got into university on her own
volition.

The strength of an electrical current.
Do not touch that wire as it carries a high voltage.

A person who offers to do a task or who does a job without being paid.
A volunteer at the hospital showed me
where to find the clinic.

Tiring, by monotony or length.
Some people say it is wearisome to travel across the prairies because there is little to break up the monotony.

To show that one is pleased by someone’s arrival.
The whole family came to the door to
welcome their guests.

In boxing, weighing between 63.5 and 67 kilograms.
He competed in the welterweight
division of the boxing tournament.

Wonderful; remarkable; admirable.
The rainbow was a wondrous sight.

Causing anxiety or concern.
Waiting for the laboratory results was
worrisome.

Abominable snowman.
There was another reported sighting of a yeti in the mountains.

A variety of summer squash.
Our garden produces lots of zucchini.
